About

Kate Kallot is a technologist and deep tech founder recognized globally for pioneering equitable access to advanced technologies across emerging markets. She is the Founder & CEO of Amini, a data infrastructure company building sovereign data and compute systems for Africa and the Global South. Previously, Kate held senior leadership roles at NVIDIA, where she led global developer relations and ecosystem expansion across underserved geographies, and Arm, where she was instrumental in scaling the TinyML movement, enabling machine learning on ultra-low-power edge devices. At Intel, she spearheaded the development of the Neural Compute Stick, the world’s first USB-based AI development kit, democratizing computer vision and edge AI for millions of developers worldwide.Her work has been recognized as a 2025 TIME100 Impact Award honoree, a TIME100 Most Influential People in AI honoree, a World Economic Forum Tech Pioneer, and a One Young World Entrepreneur of the Year 2024. A trusted voice in global AI policy and digital equity, Kate serves as Vice Chair of the ICC Global Environmental and Energy Commission and is a member of EY’s Global AI Advisory Council. I do not respond to LinkedIn messages for hiring inquiries, instead please contact info@amini.ai

Featured

  • Post

    I’m honored and humbled to share that I’ve been named a recipient of the TIME100 Impact Awards alongside trailblazers such as Alexis Ohanian Sr., Jamie Salter, and Zak Brown. But if I’m honest, this moment feels less like a recognition and more like a homecoming.

    I chose to leave big tech and go home 3 years ago. Little did I know that "going home" would mean building AI infrastructure across Africa and the Global South. That it would mean fighting for data sovereignty, for compute access, and for the right of our nations to refine their own intelligence rather than export it raw. It means believing that our people deserve to see themselves in the future.

    I’m grateful to TIME for this recognition, and even more thankful to my family, my team at Amini, the communities, governments, partners, and young developers who prove every day that the Global Majority is not waiting to be invited into the AI age. This award belongs to all of you.

    Thank you TIME Sam Jacobs Harry Booth Jessica Sibley!

    Kate Kallot Is Building AI for the Global South

    time.com

    647 · 76 comments

  • Post

    Forbes cover this month! Look at God 🙌

    I feel incredibly grateful for the past two years and excited for what's to come. Our mission is nothing short of small, and we're committed to driving the positive change we want to see in Africa and the Global South. Thank you to all our investors, partners, and supporters;     Amini is only getting started, and we have some exciting news to share with the world very soon 🚀 🌍

    https://lnkd.in/dUChCnP6

    #Forbes #Cover #ForbesAfrica #Grateful #AI #Africa

    1,119 · 137 comments

  • Post

    Really honored to be featured in TIME's list of the 100 most influential people in AI 2023! So proud to see our work at Amini and our vision for Africa highlighted here along the many people I've worked with and admire!
    #TIME100AI #PuttingAfricaOnTheMap 🚀 🛰 🌍

    TIME100 AI 2023: Kate Kallot

    time.com

    663 · 131 comments

  • Post

    As Brazil prepares to host the nineteenth G20 Summit, I am deeply honored to announce that I have been selected to join the B20's Integrity & Compliance Task Force. This opportunity reflects the values we champion in the technology sector but also places us at the forefront of the global economic dialogue.

    Why Does the B20 Matter? 📈
    As the official dialogue forum for the G20 to engage with the global business community, the B20 is instrumental in shaping policies to tackle the world's most pressing economic challenges. Since its establishment in 2010, it has served as a vital platform for business leaders to build consensus and set priorities, aiming to influence global economic policies with impactful recommendations.

    My Mission on the Task Force 🌱
    In this task force, I am eager to contribute to global standards that resonate with our mission at Amini. My focus will be on ensuring that global regulations and compliance frameworks do not leave anyone behind, advocating for data integrity, transparency, and equitable market access. This aligns with the B20's mission of fostering inclusive growth and sustainable development, principles that guide our work in promoting ethical leadership and environmental stewardship.

    Excited to join forces with global peers, aiming to drive G20 recommendations for a sustainable future through data. 🤝

    304 · 37 comments

  • Post

    Big day at Amini!

    Excited to announce that we've secured a thrilling $4M Seed funding, led by     Salesforce Ventures and Female Founders Fund and with participation from Pale blue dot, Superorganism and Satgana.

    Really proud of our team's passion and creativity that's driving us forward.

    Together, we're shaping a future of resilience for 1 billion people by 2030.

    Excited for what's ahead – we’re moving onwards and forward! 🌱🌟
    🔗 https://lnkd.in/eRt_wTdD


    #Amini #Leadership #Innovation #Sustainability #TechForGood

    Salesforce Ventures and Female Founders Fund back Kenyan climate-tech Amini in $4M seed round

    Google Cloud

    3,203,924 followers

    1w •

    Meet Kate Kallot, CEO and founder of Kenyan AI company, Amini. She’s been named as one of TIME Magazine's 100 most influential people in AI, so it's safe to say she knows her stuff. As someone who's self-taught in tech, she knows what’s really possible if you find the right learning online.

    Where do you see the biggest potential for AI?

    Where do you see the biggest potential for AI?

  • Kate Kallot reposted this

    Alaa Murabit

    • 3rd+

    Physician. Capital architect. Founder: Voice of Libyan Women during Libyan Revolution. Created & launched For Mama (now Every Pregnancy), first Muslim collaborative for maternal health. SDG co-architect. $40B+ mobilized.

    View my newsletter

    2w •

    The localization agenda localized labor. It never localized capital or power.

    Local organizations face compliance burdens designed for 50-person finance teams, but lack the capital or authority to decide how to move them. We're just subcontracting with better language.

    I've lived this arc. I built Voice of Libyan Women during a civil war by working with faith leaders, not around them. That same logic, start with the community's own moral authority and capital traditions, led me to create what's now Every Pregnancy, the first zakat-approved philanthropic collaborative for maternal health. It raised $34 million in two Ramadans and over $25 million so far this Ramadan. The architecture works because it was built from the capital source outward, not retrofitted to fit donor infrastructure.

    Islamic financing mobilizes hundreds of billions of dollars annually in the countries where mothers and children are most likely to die from preventable causes. The sector's refusal to integrate them isn't a cultural competency gap. It's a technical failure that costs lives.

    Full piece in     Devex 👇
    https://lnkd.in/eJNwikas

    The localization agenda localized labor. It forgot to localize capitalThe localization agenda localized labor. It forgot to localize capital

    devex.com

    44 reactions44

    1 comment1 comment

    •

    4 reposts4 reposts

    Comment
    Send

  • Kate Kallot

    • 3rd+

    Founder & CEO | Building Sovereign Data Infrastructure for the Global South | TIME100 AI | WEF Tech Pioneer

    2w •

    Honored to be delivering the keynote at #JUMPSTARTER2026 powered by the Alibaba Entrepreneurs Fund.

    The AI race is accelerating, but the real divide emerging today is between countries building the infrastructure behind intelligence and those still relying on systems they do not control. #Data, #connectivity, and #compute are becoming the new levers of economic power.

    Nations that fail to secure them will not simply fall behind; they risk losing the ability to participate on their own terms. Our work in the Global South is centered on changing that narrative, giving countries the choice, agency, and capacity to build sovereign, equitable AI futures.

    If this resonates with you and you’re building for or within the Global South, I’d love to continue the conversation. Comment below or book a meeting here: https://www.amini.ai/demo

    Amini #AI #DeepTech #Innovation #DataSovereignty

    178 reactions178

  • Kate Kallot

    • 3rd+

    Founder & CEO | Building Sovereign Data Infrastructure for the Global South | TIME100 AI | WEF Tech Pioneer

    3w • Edited •

    The future of AI in the Global South must go beyond adoption to ownership.

    Financing local infrastructure is key to ensuring countries retain both data sovereignty and economic value.

    Great conversation with two of my favorite humans     Jonathan Reid and Alaa Murabit brought together by Catherine Cheney at Devex on what this could look like in practice.

    Devex

    120,846 followers

    3w • Edited •

    Jonathan Reid, the Barbadian minister of innovation, industry, science, and technology, joins Devex along with Amini founder Kate Kallot and 500 Global's Alaa Murabit to discuss how small countries can take back financial and data sovereignty over AI.

    #AI #datasovereignity #globaldevelopment #podcast

    How the global south can finance AI infrastructure on its own termsHow the global south can finance AI infrastructure on its own terms

    devex.com

    40 reactions40

  • Kate Kallot reposted this

    Luca Bucken

    • 3rd+

    Executive Director, Government Relations @ 500 Global | Board Member | Founder | Expert Consultant

    1mo • Edited •

    Thrilled to be part of the 500 Global team at such a pivotal moment. Talent lives everywhere, but capital and opportunity do not. Working with governments across the globe towards mobilizing sustainable investment into the markets that require it most, where innovation and impact intersect, is a mission I'm deeply committed to. A sincere thank you to Courtney Powell, Christine Tsai, Alaa Murabit, Bedy Yang, Mei Chel Tan, and the rest of 500 Global's leadership for the trust and confidence.

    500 Global

    369,929 followers

    1mo •

    We’re pleased to welcome Luca Bucken to 500 Global as our new Executive Director, Government Relations.

    Based in Abu Dhabi, Luca will lead our government relations efforts, working closely with sovereign partners to advance public–private partnerships that align capital, policy, and ecosystem development with national priorities — particularly across digital transformation, AI, and economic diversification.

    We’re excited to have Luca on board as we deepen our engagement with governments around the world to uplift people and economies through entrepreneurship. Read more here: https://lnkd.in/gQvdk8e8

    Courtney Powell | Alaa Murabit | Anna Heng | Mei Chel Tan | Carrie Liauw | Bedy Yang | Christine Tsai | Paul E. Yoo

    214 reactions214

  • Kate Kallot

    • 3rd+

    Founder & CEO | Building Sovereign Data Infrastructure for the Global South | TIME100 AI | WEF Tech Pioneer

    1mo •

    𝗔𝗜 𝗶𝘀 𝗯𝗲𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗱𝗲𝗳𝗮𝘂𝗹𝘁 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗺𝗮𝗻𝘆, 𝘆𝗲𝘁 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗶𝗻𝗳𝗿𝗮𝘀𝘁𝗿𝘂𝗰𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗲 𝗽𝗼𝘄𝗲𝗿𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗶𝘁 𝗿𝗲𝗺𝗮𝗶𝗻𝘀 𝗵𝗶𝗴𝗵𝗹𝘆 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝗰𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗲𝗱. Without building their own data, connectivity, and compute capacity, entire regions risk remaining consumers in an economy they actively fuel.

    Next week, I’ll be in Barcelona for     MWC Barcelona, contributing to discussions on AI infrastructure and where the Global Majority stands.

    On the main stage, I’ll deliver our keynote: 𝗕𝘂𝗶𝗹𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗜𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗶𝗴𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗪𝗼𝗿𝗹𝗱, a reflection on how we move from consuming AI to actively participating in the AI Economy.

    Our team will also be at the AI-RAN Alliance booth (2E45), demonstrating how Amini Cloud extends AI into the network layer. 𝗖𝗼𝗺𝗲 𝘃𝗶𝘀𝗶𝘁 𝘂𝘀 𝘄𝗵𝗶𝗹𝗲 𝘆𝗼𝘂’𝗿𝗲 𝗮𝘁 MWC Barcelona.

    𝗜𝗳 𝘆𝗼𝘂'𝗿𝗲 𝘄𝗼𝗿𝗸𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗮𝗰𝗿𝗼𝘀𝘀 𝗱𝗮𝘁𝗮 𝗶𝗻𝗳𝗿𝗮𝘀𝘁𝗿𝘂𝗰𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗲, 𝗮𝗱𝘃𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲𝗱 𝘁𝗲𝗹𝗲𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗺𝘂𝗻𝗶𝗰𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀, 𝗱𝗶𝗴𝗶𝘁𝗮𝗹 𝗽𝘂𝗯𝗹𝗶𝗰 𝘀𝘆𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗺𝘀, 𝗼𝗿 𝗻𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗹 𝗶𝗻𝗻𝗼𝘃𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝘀𝘁𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗲𝗴𝘆, 𝗜’𝗱 𝗹𝗼𝘃𝗲 𝘁𝗼 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝗻𝗲𝗰𝘁 𝗶𝗻 𝗕𝗮𝗿𝗰𝗲𝗹𝗼𝗻𝗮.

    📩 Reach out: https://lnkd.in/eZHTuVSw
    𝗦𝗲𝗲 𝘆𝗼𝘂 𝗮𝘁 𝗠𝗪𝗖.

  • Kate Kallot reposted this

    MWC Barcelona

    64,808 followers

    1mo • Edited •

    Mark your calendars for our Day 2️⃣ Keynotes on Tuesday, 3 March! 📅

    𝗞𝗲𝘆𝗻𝗼𝘁𝗲 𝟲: 𝗔𝗿𝗰𝗵𝗶𝘁𝗲𝗰𝘁𝘀 𝗼𝗳 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗔𝗜 𝗔𝗴𝗲
    This session will bring together pioneering leaders shaping both the systems, and the consequences of the AI world being built today.

    ✅ Speakers include:     Cristiano R. Amon (Qualcomm) | Michael Weening (Calix) + more

    𝗞𝗲𝘆𝗻𝗼𝘁𝗲 𝟳: 𝗣𝗲𝗲𝗿𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗜𝗻𝘁𝗼 𝗖𝘂𝗹𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗲'𝘀 𝗖𝗿𝘆𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗹 𝗕𝗮𝗹𝗹
    Visionary leaders will examine how innovation is transforming not only what we build, but how we live, work, and connect.

    ✅ Speakers include: Cathy Hackl (Future Dynamics) | Azeem Azhar (Exponential View) | Andrew Feinberg (Netcracker Technology) | Rachel Thornton (Adobe) | Johanna Faries (Blizzard Entertainment)

    𝗞𝗲𝘆𝗻𝗼𝘁𝗲 𝟴: 𝗕𝘂𝗶𝗹𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗜𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗶𝗴𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗪𝗼𝗿𝗹𝗱
    Join leaders shaping the future across personal, societal, and enterprise realms: smart wearables that empower individual health choices, AI solutions that bridge economic and technological divides, and next-layer network infrastructure that allows organisations the reach and speed to do more.

    ✅ Speakers include: Justin Springham (Mobile World Live) | Kate Kallot (Amini) | Tom Hale (ŌURA) | Kate Johnson (Lumen Technologies)

    𝗞𝗲𝘆𝗻𝗼𝘁𝗲 𝟵: 𝗕𝘂𝗶𝗹𝘁 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗪𝗵𝗮𝘁’𝘀 𝗡𝗲𝘅𝘁
    This session will explore how AI, cloud-native technologies and next-generation infrastructure are reshaping not only how networks operate, but how people experience the digital world.

    ✅ Speakers include: Ralph Mupita (MTN) | Pietro Labriola (TIM) | Ali Taha Koç (Turkcell) | Keri Gilder (Colt Technology Services)

    Buy yours Leaders Conference/VIP pass and make sure you’re in the room to catch all the future-defining insights as they happen 🎟️ https://gsma.at/135

    Already secured your pass? Explore the agenda and start planning your #MWC26 experience 👉 https://gsma.at/136

    • 3rd+

    Founder & CEO | Building Sovereign Data Infrastructure for the Global South | TIME100 AI | WEF Tech Pioneer

    1mo • Edited •

    🇮🇳 Landed in New Delhi today for the India AI Impact Summit!

    I’ll be joining the main event panel “Beyond Digital Empires: AI Implementation for Middle Powers” to unpack how nations can build national AI capabilities without surrendering sovereignty, leverage, or long-term economic value.

    On Thursday, at the South to South Dialog hosted by our partners     Qhala we will go deeper: from Delhi to Brasilia to Nairobi, what does real collaboration between emerging powers look like in the AI era?

    If you are in Delhi and care about building sovereign, inclusive AI ecosystems, let's connect:
    https://lnkd.in/eZHTuVSw
    Amini

    • 3rd+

    Founder & CEO | Building Sovereign Data Infrastructure for the Global South | TIME100 AI | WEF Tech Pioneer

    2mo •

    Best community I’ve been part of. If you’re a crazy Founder, would definitely recommend you to apply!

    Verena Kuhn

    • 3rd+

    🚀 Head of Innovator Communities, WEF | Connecting Startups, VCs & Policymakers | ex-P&G

    2mo •

    🛫Last call for all the crazy Founders building at the frontier: this opportunity closes in 3 days!

    If you're at Series A with breakthrough technology and thinking seriously about scale, impact, and shaping how your industry evolves, the World Economic Forum's Technology Pioneers Community is for you!

    What it is: A world class founder community and free two-year WEF engagement that gives early-stage founders a seat at tables that usually require years more traction: conversations with policymakers, big industry players, and civil society.

    Who is in the community: Founders that push the boundaries - putting data centers in space (yes, talking about you     Philip Johnston), making fusion work (Francesco Sciortino) and other things that most people deem impossible across AI, climate, space, frontier science, and industrial innovation, many who have gone on to build category-defining companies.

    Current Technology Pioneers include:
    👉 Kian Katanforoosh (Workera)
    👉 Kate Kallot (Amini)
    👉 Anton Osika (Lovable)
    👉 Pierre Baqué (Neural Concept)
    👉 Chad Edwards (CuspAI)
    👉 Jae-Pil Park (Nara Space)
    👉 Srinath Ravichandran (AgniKul Cosmos)
    👉 Haojun Jia (Deep Principle)

    The ask: 15 minutes to apply. The upside will last for years.
    📅 Deadline: January 31, 2026 🔗 Apply here: https://lnkd.in/ekDZeb_5

    #TechPioneers #Founders #Startups #WorldEconomicForum #FrontierTech #Innovation
